SEARCH
Package server.controller

Class WaitingListCheckThread

java.lang.Object
java.lang.Thread
server.controller.WaitingListCheckThread
All Implemented Interfaces:
Runnable
public class WaitingListCheckThread extends Thread
Background thread that periodically checks the Waiting List to see if any pending requests can be promoted to active orders based on table availability.

      public WaitingListCheckThread()

  • Method Details

    • run

      public void run()
      Main execution loop of the thread. Sleeps for a defined interval and then triggers the waiting list processing.
      Specified by:
      run in interface Runnable
      Overrides:
      run in class Thread

    • processWaitingList

      private void processWaitingList()
      Core logic for processing the waiting list. Iterates through all waiting customers and checks if a table is available. It accounts for: 1. Physical table capacity. 2. Existing orders in the DB. 3. Orders promoted *during this specific cycle* (to prevent double-booking).

    • isOverlapping

      private boolean isOverlapping(Date date1, Date date2)
      Checks if two dates overlap based on the standard order duration (2 hours).
      Parameters:
      date1 - First date.
      date2 - Second date.
      Returns:
      true if the time windows overlap.

    • isValidRemoveWaitingList

      private boolean isValidRemoveWaitingList(List<Integer> tableList, List<Integer> orders)
      valid that if we add the new order (implicitly handled by the caller logic) we still have room. The logic sorts both lists in reverse order (Largest first) and attempts to match.
      Parameters:
      tableList - List of all physical table capacities.
      orders - List of current active order sizes (guest counts).
      Returns:
      true if the allocation is valid.

    • promoteEntry

      private boolean promoteEntry(WaitingList entry)
      Delegates the actual promotion logic to the controller.
      Parameters:
      entry - The waiting list entry to promote.
      Returns:
      true if successful.

    • stopThread

      public void stopThread()
      Stops the thread safely.
